- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Project Architecture Guide

## Stack
- Java 21 · Spring Boot 3.x · Spring Cloud
- JPA/Hibernate · PostgreSQL · Redis
- Liquibase · Maven · Docker · Kubernetes
- OpenTelemetry · Prometheus · Grafana · Jaeger

## Architecture: Clean + Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters)

### Package Structure
Each microservice follows this layout:
```
com.company.{service}/
├── domain/
│   ├── model/          # Pure domain entities & value objects
│   ├── port/
│   │   ├── in/         # Use-case interfaces (driving ports)
│   │   └── out/        # Repository/external interfaces (driven ports)
│   └── service/        # Domain services & use-case implementations
├── application/
│   └── usecase/        # Application services orchestrating domain
├── adapter/
│   ├── in/
│   │   ├── web/        # REST controllers (@RestController)
│   │   └── messaging/  # Kafka/RabbitMQ consumers
│   └── out/
│       ├── persistence/ # JPA repositories & entities
│       ├── cache/       # Redis adapters
│       └── client/      # Feign/WebClient outbound calls
└── config/             # Spring @Configuration classes
```

## Strict Rules

### Domain Layer
- ZERO Spring annotations inside `domain/` package
- Domain entities must NOT extend JPA entities
- Value Objects are immutable (use Java records)
- No `Optional` returns in domain ports — use domain exceptions
- Business rules live ONLY in domain services

### Adapter Layer
- JPA entities live in `adapter/out/persistence/` ONLY
- Always map between JPA entities ↔ domain models via mappers
- Use MapStruct for all mappings (never manual getters)
- Redis keys follow pattern: `{service}:{entity}:{id}`

### Application Layer
- Use `@Transactional` only in application services, never domain
- Each use case = one method in one application service class
- Return domain models, never JPA entities or DTOs from use cases

## Coding Standards
- Java 21 features: records, sealed classes, pattern matching, virtual threads
- Use `var` for local variables when type is obvious
- Prefer constructor injection (never @Autowired on fields)
- All public methods in ports must have Javadoc

## Error Handling
- Domain exceptions extend `DomainException` (unchecked)
- HTTP errors mapped in `@RestControllerAdvice` adapter only
- Never catch-and-swallow exceptions in domain or application layers

## Testing Pyramid
- Unit tests: domain services & use cases (no Spring context)
- Integration tests: adapter layer with @SpringBootTest + Testcontainers
- Use Hexagonal test slices: `@WebMvcTest` for web adapters only

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">

    <!-- 
       - POM Declaration.
       -->
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
    <parent>
        <groupId>net.momcorp</groupId>
        <artifactId>net.momcorp.bom</artifactId>
        <version>1.0.18-RELEASE</version>
    </parent>

    <artifactId>momcorp-commons-ms</artifactId>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>
    <version>1.0.0-RELEASE</version>
    <description>Momcorp commons microservices.</description>

    <!--
       - Properties section.
       -->
    <properties>
        <commons-lang.version>1.0.11-RELEASE</commons-lang.version>
        <commons-component.version>1.0.11-RELEASE</commons-component.version>
        <repository-lib.version>1.0.11-RELEASE</repository-lib.version>
        <commons-service.version>1.0.11-RELEASE</commons-service.version>
    </properties>


    <!--
       - Repository definitions.
       -->
    <distributionManagement>
        <repository>
            <id>github</id>
            <name>GitHub momcorp Apache Maven Packages</name>
            <url>https://maven.pkg.github.com/momcorp-net/momcorp-commons-ms</url>
        </repository>
    </distributionManagement>


    <!--
       - Repositories section.
       -->
    <repositories>
        <repository>
            <id>github</id>
            <name>momcorp-maven-bom</name>
            <url>https://maven.pkg.github.com/momcorp-net/momcorp-maven-bom</url>
        </repository>
    </repositories>


    <!--
       - Dependencies section.
       -->
    <dependencies>

        <!--
           - Test dependencies.
           -->

        <!-- Spring Boot Starter Test (includes JUnit 5, Mockito, MockMvc, etc.) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>


        <!--
           - Build dependencies.
           -->

        <!--
           - momcorp dependencies.
           -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>net.momcorp</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-lang</artifactId>
            <version>${commons-lang.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>net.momcorp</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-component</artifactId>
            <version>${commons-component.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>net.momcorp</groupId>
            <artifactId>repository-lib</artifactId>
            <version>${repository-lib.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>net.momcorp</groupId>
            <artifactId>commons-service</artifactId>
            <version>${commons-service.version}</version>
        </dependency>


        <!--
           - Springboot dependencies.
           -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-redis</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springdoc</groupId>
            <artifactId>springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui</artifactId>
        </dependency>


        <!--
           - Hibernate dependencies.
           -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hibernate.validator</groupId>
            <artifactId>hibernate-validator</artifactId>
        </dependency>


        <!--
           -Database dependencies.
           -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId>
            <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId>
        </dependency>


        <!-- Lombok dependencies. -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
        </dependency>

    </dependencies>


    <!--
       - Build section. 
       -->
    <build>

        <finalName>${project.artifactId}</finalName>


        <!--
           - Resources section for filtering properties and YAML files.
           -->
        <resources>
            <resource>
                <directory>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources</directory>
                <filtering>true</filtering>
                <includes>
                    <include>**/*.properties</include>
                    <include>**/*.yml</include>
                </includes>
            </resource>
        </resources>


        <!--
           - Plugins section.
           -->
        <plugins>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <source>${java.version}</source>
                    <target>${java.version}</target>
                    <compilerArgs>
                        <arg>-parameters</arg>
                    </compilerArgs>
                    <annotationProcessorPaths>
                        <path>
                            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                            <version>${lombok.version}</version>
                        </path>
                    </annotationProcessorPaths>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-resources-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <encoding>${project.build.sourceEncoding}</encoding>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-clean-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <excludes>
                        <exclude>**/*IT.java</exclude>
                    </excludes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-source-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>attach-sources</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>jar</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-javadoc-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>attach-javadocs</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>jar</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-deploy-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

        </plugins>

    </build>

</project>

I have completed a thorough exploration of the momcorp-insurance-core project. This is a **microservices-based insurance platform** using **Domain-Driven Design (DDD)** and **Hexagonal Architecture** principles. Below is a complete structural breakdown and pattern documentation to help you replicate patterns for a new microservice.

---

#### 3.3 **Shared Domain Base Entity**

All domain entities must extend this base class:

```java
package com.momcorp.insurance.shared.domain.model;

import java.time.OffsetDateTime;
import java.util.UUID;

/**
 * Base entity contract for all domain aggregates.
 */
public abstract class BaseEntity {

    private UUID id;
    private OffsetDateTime createdAt;
    private OffsetDateTime updatedAt;
    private Long version;
    private String createdBy;
    private String updatedBy;
    private OffsetDateTime deletedAt;
    private boolean deleted;

    public UUID getId() { return id; }
    public void setId(final UUID id) { this.id = id; }

    public OffsetDateTime getCreatedAt() { return createdAt; }
    public void setCreatedAt(final OffsetDateTime createdAt) { this.createdAt = createdAt; }

    public OffsetDateTime getUpdatedAt() { return updatedAt; }
    public void setUpdatedAt(final OffsetDateTime updatedAt) { this.updatedAt = updatedAt; }

    public Long getVersion() { return version; }
    public void setVersion(final Long version) { this.version = version; }

    public String getCreatedBy() { return createdBy; }
    public void setCreatedBy(final String createdBy) { this.createdBy = createdBy; }

    public String getUpdatedBy() { return updatedBy; }
    public void setUpdatedBy(final String updatedBy) { this.updatedBy = updatedBy; }

    public OffsetDateTime getDeletedAt() { return deletedAt; }
    public void setDeletedAt(final OffsetDateTime deletedAt) { this.deletedAt = deletedAt; }

    public boolean isDeleted() { return deleted; }
    public void setDeleted(final boolean deleted) { this.deleted = deleted; }
}
```

**Key patterns:**
- Uses UUID for IDs (distributed systems)
- OffsetDateTime for all timestamps (timezone-aware)
- Version field for optimistic locking
- Audit fields: createdBy, updatedBy
- Soft delete support: deletedAt, deleted flag

    public void setVersion(final Long version) { this.version = version; }

    public String getStatus() { return status; }
    public void setStatus(final String status) { this.status = status; }
}

### 7. HEXAGONAL ARCHITECTURE LAYER BREAKDOWN

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    PRESENTATION LAYER                       │
│         (infrastructure/web → REST Controllers)             │
│  Converts HTTP requests/responses to/from domain objects    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   APPLICATION LAYER                         │
│         (application → Use Cases/Services)                  │
│  Orchestrates domain logic, coordinates cross-service calls │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     DOMAIN LAYER                            │
│     (domain/model → Aggregates, Value Objects, Events)      │
│    Contains pure business logic (zero dependencies)         │
│                                                             │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  Ports (domain/port)                               │   │
│  │  - Outbound ports: interfaces defining contracts   │   │
│  │  - Inbound ports: contracts for use cases          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  INFRASTRUCTURE LAYER                       │
│  (infrastructure → Adapters, Configuration)                │
│                                                             │
│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐               │
│  │  Persistence     │  │  Web Adapter     │               │
│  │  (repositories,  │  │  (controllers)   │               │
│  │   entities,      │  │                  │               │
│  │   mappers)       │  │                  │               │
│  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘               │
│                                                             │
│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐               │
│  │  Messaging       │  │  Configuration   │               │
│  │  (event pub/sub) │  │  (Spring config) │               │
│  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Per-package responsibilities:**

| Package | Layer | Responsibility |
|---------|-------|-----------------|
| `com.momcorp.insurance.product` | Root | Package bootstrap |
| `domain.model` | Domain | Aggregates, value objects, entities |
| `domain.port` | Domain | Port interfaces (Repository, Publisher, etc.) |
| `application` | Application | Use cases, service orchestration |
| `infrastructure.web` | Adapter | REST Controllers, request/response mappers |
| `infrastructure.persistence` | Adapter | JPA Entities, Repositories, DB mappers |
| `infrastructure.messaging` | Adapter | RabbitMQ publishers/consumers |
| `infrastructure.config` | Adapter | Spring beans, configuration classes |

Six bounded contexts, each with complete independence:

**Database schema per service:**

- All inter-service communication via REST or RabbitMQ

### 9. COMMUNICATION PATTERNS

**Synchronous (REST):**
```
Inbound: Service A → REST Controller → Application → Domain
Outbound: Domain → Application → Service B REST endpoint
```

**Asynchronous (RabbitMQ):**
```
Publishing: Domain Event → Application → Messaging Adapter → RabbitMQ Topic Exchange
Consuming: RabbitMQ Topic Queue → Messaging Adapter → Application → Domain Event Handler
```

**Event naming convention:** `{domain}.{entity}.{event}`
- Example: `product.policy.created`, `claims.claim.approved`

### 12. CRITICAL ARCHITECTURE RULES (FROM AGENTS.MD)

1. **No code duplication between services** - Create shared library if needed
2. **All classes documented with Javadoc** - Every public class must have docs
3. **English only** - All code, comments, logs in English
4. **Single shared database** - Different schemas per service
5. **No direct cross-schema DB access** - Only via REST or messaging
6. **No hardcoded values** - All config externalized
7. **i18n for all messages** - Exceptions, logs, user messages
8. **MDC for distributed tracing** - Use correlationId, serviceName, userId
9. **Soft deletes** - Use deletedAt + deleted flag instead of hard deletes
10. **Optimistic locking** - Use version field for concurrency control
11. **Versioned APIs** - All endpoints start with `/api/v1/`

#### 3.3 **Shared Domain Base Entity**
**Path:** `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/shared/mom-shared-domain/src/main/java/com/momcorp/insurance/shared/domain/model/BaseEntity.java`

All domain entities must extend this base class:

```java
package com.momcorp.insurance.shared.domain.model;

import java.time.OffsetDateTime;
import java.util.UUID;

/**
 * Base entity contract for all domain aggregates.
 */
public abstract class BaseEntity {

    private UUID id;
    private OffsetDateTime createdAt;
    private OffsetDateTime updatedAt;
    private Long version;
    private String createdBy;
    private String updatedBy;
    private OffsetDateTime deletedAt;
    private boolean deleted;

    public UUID getId() { return id; }
    public void setId(final UUID id) { this.id = id; }

    public OffsetDateTime getCreatedAt() { return createdAt; }
    public void setCreatedAt(final OffsetDateTime createdAt) { this.createdAt = createdAt; }

    public OffsetDateTime getUpdatedAt() { return updatedAt; }
    public void setUpdatedAt(final OffsetDateTime updatedAt) { this.updatedAt = updatedAt; }

    public Long getVersion() { return version; }
    public void setVersion(final Long version) { this.version = version; }

    public String getCreatedBy() { return createdBy; }
    public void setCreatedBy(final String createdBy) { this.createdBy = createdBy; }

    public String getUpdatedBy() { return updatedBy; }
    public void setUpdatedBy(final String updatedBy) { this.updatedBy = updatedBy; }

    public OffsetDateTime getDeletedAt() { return deletedAt; }
    public void setDeletedAt(final OffsetDateTime deletedAt) { this.deletedAt = deletedAt; }

    public boolean isDeleted() { return deleted; }
    public void setDeleted(final boolean deleted) { this.deleted = deleted; }
}
```

**Key patterns:**
- Uses UUID for IDs (distributed systems)
- OffsetDateTime for all timestamps (timezone-aware)
- Version field for optimistic locking
- Audit fields: createdBy, updatedBy
- Soft delete support: deletedAt, deleted flag

#### 3.4 **Shared API Base DTO**

All API DTOs must extend this base class:

```java
package com.momcorp.insurance.shared.api.dto;

import jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull;
import java.io.Serial;
import java.io.Serializable;
import java.time.OffsetDateTime;
import java.util.UUID;

/**
 * Base DTO contract shared across service APIs.
 */
public abstract class BaseDto implements Serializable {

    @Serial
    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;

    @NotNull
    private UUID id;

    @NotNull
    private OffsetDateTime createdAt;

    @NotNull
    private OffsetDateTime updatedAt;

    @NotNull
    private Long version;

    private String status;

    // Getters and setters
    public UUID getId() { return id; }
    public void setId(final UUID id) { this.id = id; }

    public OffsetDateTime getCreatedAt() { return createdAt; }
    public void setCreatedAt(final OffsetDateTime createdAt) { this.createdAt = createdAt; }

    public OffsetDateTime getUpdatedAt() { return updatedAt; }
    public void setUpdatedAt(final OffsetDateTime updatedAt) { this.updatedAt = updatedAt; }

    public Long getVersion() { return version; }
    public void setVersion(final Long version) { this.version = version; }

    public String getStatus() { return status; }
    public void setStatus(final String status) { this.status = status; }
}
```

**Key patterns:**
- Implements Serializable with serialVersionUID
- All shared fields use @NotNull validation
- Status field for common state management

      file: db/changelog/services/db.changelog-operations.yaml
  - include:
      file: db/changelog/services/db.changelog-claims.yaml
  - include:
      file: db/changelog/services/db.changelog-administration.yaml
  - include:
      file: db/changelog/services/db.changelog-reinsurance.yaml
```

#### 6.2 **Service Changelog Template**
**Path:** `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/liquibase/mom-liquibase/src/main/resources/db/changelog/services/db.changelog-product.yaml`

```yaml
databaseChangeLog:
  - changeSet:
      id: product-0001-create-schema
      author: codex
      changes:
        - sql:
            sql: CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS product;
```

**Pattern for new services:**
- Create one YAML file per service schema
- Use naming convention: `db.changelog-{service-name}.yaml`
- Use IDs: `{service-name}-{sequence:04d}-{description}`
- Always use `IF NOT EXISTS` to ensure idempotency

---

### 7. HEXAGONAL ARCHITECTURE LAYER BREAKDOWN

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    PRESENTATION LAYER                       │
│         (infrastructure/web → REST Controllers)             │
│  Converts HTTP requests/responses to/from domain objects    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   APPLICATION LAYER                         │
│         (application → Use Cases/Services)                  │
│  Orchestrates domain logic, coordinates cross-service calls │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     DOMAIN LAYER                            │
│     (domain/model → Aggregates, Value Objects, Events)      │
│    Contains pure business logic (zero dependencies)         │
│                                                             │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  Ports (domain/port)                               │   │
│  │  - Outbound ports: interfaces defining contracts   │   │
│  │  - Inbound ports: contracts for use cases          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  INFRASTRUCTURE LAYER                       │
│  (infrastructure → Adapters, Configuration)                │
│                                                             │
│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐               │
│  │  Persistence     │  │  Web Adapter     │               │
│  │  (repositories,  │  │  (controllers)   │               │
│  │   entities,      │  │                  │               │
│  │   mappers)       │  │                  │               │
│  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘               │
│                                                             │
│  ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐               │
│  │  Messaging       │  │  Configuration   │               │
│  │  (event pub/sub) │  │  (Spring config) │               │
│  └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Per-package responsibilities:**

| Package | Layer | Responsibility |
|---------|-------|-----------------|
| `com.momcorp.insurance.product` | Root | Package bootstrap |
| `domain.model` | Domain | Aggregates, value objects, entities |
| `domain.port` | Domain | Port interfaces (Repository, Publisher, etc.) |
| `application` | Application | Use cases, service orchestration |
| `infrastructure.web` | Adapter | REST Controllers, request/response mappers |
| `infrastructure.persistence` | Adapter | JPA Entities, Repositories, DB mappers |
| `infrastructure.messaging` | Adapter | RabbitMQ publishers/consumers |
| `infrastructure.config` | Adapter | Spring beans, configuration classes |

---

### 8. MICROSERVICES DEFINITION

Six bounded contexts, each with complete independence:

| Service | Port | Package | Responsibility |
|---------|------|---------|-----------------|
| **mom-product-service** | 8081 | `com.momcorp.insurance.product` | Product definition, pricing, underwriting config |
| **mom-commons-service** | 8082 | `com.momcorp.insurance.commons` | Shared capabilities (customers, documents, workflows) |
| **mom-operations-service** | 8083 | `com.momcorp.insurance.operations` | Policy lifecycle (quote, issue, renew, cancel, endorse) |
| **mom-claims-service** | 8084 | `com.momcorp.insurance.claims` | Claims management (submission, assessment, approval, payment) |
| **mom-administration-service** | 8085 | `com.momcorp.insurance.administration` | Access control, governance, audit, compliance |
| **mom-reinsurance-service** | 8086 | `com.momcorp.insurance.reinsurance` | Risk transfer treaties, facultative agreements, settlements |

**Database schema per service:**
- Each service has its own schema in the shared PostgreSQL database
- NO direct cross-schema DB access between services
- All inter-service communication via REST or RabbitMQ

---

### 9. COMMUNICATION PATTERNS

**Synchronous (REST):**
```
Inbound: Service A → REST Controller → Application → Domain
Outbound: Domain → Application → Service B REST endpoint
```

**Asynchronous (RabbitMQ):**
```
Publishing: Domain Event → Application → Messaging Adapter → RabbitMQ Topic Exchange
Consuming: RabbitMQ Topic Queue → Messaging Adapter → Application → Domain Event Handler
```

**Event naming convention:** `{domain}.{entity}.{event}`
- Example: `product.policy.created`, `claims.claim.approved`

---

### 10. SHARED LIBRARIES STRUCTURE

All shared libraries are in `/shared/`:

```
shared/
├── mom-shared-api/               (1 class)
│   └── BaseDto
├── mom-shared-domain/            (1 class)
│   └── BaseEntity
├── mom-shared-observability/     (1 class)
│   └── MdcKeys
└── mom-shared-test/              (1 class)
    └── TestSupport
```

**Key principle:** Shared libraries contain ONLY foundational abstractions, NEVER business logic.

---

### 11. TECHNOLOGY STACK SUMMARY

| Category | Technology | Version |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| **Language** | Java | 21 |
| **Framework** | Spring Boot | 3.5.0 |
| **Cloud** | Spring Cloud | 2025.0.0 |
| **API Docs** | SpringDoc OpenAPI | 2.8.9 |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL | (see environment) |
| **Migration** | Liquibase | 4.31.1 |
| **Messaging** | RabbitMQ | (see environment) |
| **Testing** | JUnit 5, Mockito, Testcontainers | 1.21.0 |
| **Testing** | WireMock | 3.13.1 |
| **Code Coverage** | JaCoCo | 0.8.12 |
| **Build** | Maven | 3.x+ |

---

### 12. CRITICAL ARCHITECTURE RULES (FROM AGENTS.MD)

1. **No code duplication between services** - Create shared library if needed
2. **All classes documented with Javadoc** - Every public class must have docs
3. **English only** - All code, comments, logs in English
4. **Single shared database** - Different schemas per service
5. **No direct cross-schema DB access** - Only via REST or messaging
6. **No hardcoded values** - All config externalized
7. **i18n for all messages** - Exceptions, logs, user messages
8. **MDC for distributed tracing** - Use correlationId, serviceName, userId
9. **Soft deletes** - Use deletedAt + deleted flag instead of hard deletes
10. **Optimistic locking** - Use version field for concurrency control
11. **Versioned APIs** - All endpoints start with `/api/v1/`

---

### 13. DIRECTORY PATHS - QUICK REFERENCE

| Item | Path |
|------|------|
| **Root project** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core` |
| **Services base** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/services/` |
| **Shared libs base** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/shared/` |
| **Product service** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/services/mom-product-service/` |
| **Admin service** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/services/mom-administration-service/` |
| **Liquibase** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/liquibase/mom-liquibase/` |
| **Architecture docs** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` |
| **Domain docs** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/docs/DOMAIN_MODEL.md` |
| **Agent guidelines** | `/opt/development/git/personal/momcorp/momcorp-insurance-core/AGENTS.md` |

---

### 14. HOW TO CREATE A NEW MICROSERVICE

**To create a 7th service (e.g., `mom-billing-service`):**

1. Create directory: `/services/mom-billing-service/`
2. Copy `pom.xml` from product-service, change artifact ID to `mom-billing-service`
3. Create package hierarchy:
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.BillingServiceApplication.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.application.package-info.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.domain.model.package-info.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.domain.port.package-info.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.infrastructure.config.package-info.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.infrastructure.web.package-info.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.infrastructure.persistence.package-info.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.infrastructure.messaging.package-info.java`
   - `com.momcorp.insurance.billing.package-info.java`
4. Create test class: `BillingServiceApplicationTests.java`
5. Create config files:
   - `src/main/resources/application.yml` (set unique port)
   - `src/main/resources/i18n/messages.properties`
   - `src/test/resources/application.properties`
6. Add Liquibase changelog: `liquibase/mom-liquibase/src/main/resources/db/changelog/services/db.changelog-billing.yaml`
7. Update parent `pom.xml` to include new module

---

This comprehensive exploration provides all the patterns, structures, and content you need to replicate the hexagonal architecture for new microservices in the momcorp-insurance-core platform.

# AGENTS.md

## 1. Project Context

This project is a microservices-based insurance core system.

Follow Domain-Driven Design (DDD) principles, clean architecture, and hexagonal architecture.

Before making architectural decisions:
- ALWAYS read docs/architecture.md
- Follow it strictly

---

## 2. Architecture Rules (STRICT)

- Each domain is an independent microservice.
- Do not duplicate classes between services, if it is necessary, create a new library shared project for it. Put here all classes that are necessary for all services.
- All classes must be documented with Javadoc.
- All code must be written in English.
- All documents headers commented must have : 
  - Author
  - Email
  - Date
  - Version
  - Description
- All services are using the same database, with differents schemas.
- All database task must be done using liquibase.
- No direct DB access between services between schemas.
- Use REST for synchronous communication
- Use RabbitMQ for asynchronous communication

---

## 3. Technology Stack

- Java 21
- Spring Boot 3
- Spring Cloud
- Spring Integration
- Spring Batch
- Spring Security
- PostgreSQL
- RabbitMQ
- Docker + Kubernetes
- Maven
- Lombok
- APO
- OpenAPI (Swagger)
- Liquibase
- OpenTelemetry

---

## 4. Coding Standards

- Use Clean Architecture:
  - Controller → Service → Domain → Repository
- Use AOP:
  - Use AOP for cross-cutting concerns such as logging, security, and transaction management.
- Use DTOs for API communication.
- Rules for DTOs:
  - Prefer shared base DTO when applicable. (timestamp, version, id, status, etc)
  - All DTOs must be documented with Javadoc.
  - All DTOs must be validated.
  - All DTOs must use i18n for messages.
  - All DTOs must be serializable.
- Rules for Entities:
  - All DTOs must have a base class with common fields. (Id, createdAt, updatedAt, version, createdBy, updatedBy, deletedAt, isDeleted)
  - Use versioning for entities.
  - Use soft delete for entities.
- Do not expose entities directly.
- Use meaningful domain-driven naming.
- Follow SOLID principles.
- Follow Clean Architecture principles.
- Follow hexagonal architecture principles.
- Do not use hardcoded values, every setup must be externalized.
- Use i18n for all messages:
  - Exceptions.
  - Logs.
- Use MDC for logging.
- Use Spring Config for configuration management.
- Sensitive data must be managed using secrets.

---

## 5. API Rules

- Use RESTful APIs
- Version all endpoints (/v1/)
- Use OpenAPI (Swagger)
- Validate all inputs
- Use OpenTelemetry for tracing.
- Use Spring Security for authentication and authorization.
- Use Spring Cloud Config for configuration management.

---

## 6. Event Rules (RabbitMQ)

- Use topic exchanges
- Naming format:
  <domain>.<entity>.<event>

Examples:
- policy.policy.created
- billing.invoice.generated

- Events must be immutable
- Consumers must be idempotent

---

## 7. Testing Requirements

- Unit tests required for services
- Unit tests → Mockito
- Integration tests → MockMvc / Testcontainers
- Unit tests must use WireMock
- Integration tests required for APIs
- Integration tests must use Testcontainers
- Mock external services
- Cover main business flows

---

## 8. What NOT to do

- Do not duplicate business logic across services
- Do not bypass service boundaries
- Do not hardcode business rules

---

## 9. Development Workflow

- Read ARCHITECTURE.md and DOMAIN_MODEL.md before coding
- Implement one service at a time
- Keep changes small and focused
- Add tests with every feature

---

## 10. Logs

- Use MDC for logging.
- Logs must be in English:
  - Exceptions.
  - Logs.
- Log format:
  - json.

---

## 12. Github Actions

When pushing code on any branch:
- Run all linting.
- Run all security checks.
- Run all code quality checks.
- Run all code coverage checks.
- Run all code formatting checks.
- Run all code style checks.
- Run all code documentation checks.

When pushing code on develop branch:
- Build the project when all checks are passed.
- Publish the project on each Github package:
  - jar
  - docker image

Rules:
  
  - All artifact must be launched from a commits on release/develop branch.
  - All artifacts must be versioned with this schema:
    - jars: info is read from pom.xml.
    - docker images tags: 
      - name read from pom.xml and version read from pom.xml.
      - allway create the latest tag as well.  
  - All github actions must be documented in .github/workflows/ folder.
  - Do not use hardcoded values in any github action.
  - Use secrets and variables in all github action.
  - All github actions must be idempotent.
  - All can be triggered manually, with inputs if it is necessary and must be approved before running.

---

## 13. Liquiabse rules

  - Must be one unique liquibase project per all services.

---

## 14. Service Boundaries (STRICT)

- product-service → product definition only
- operations-service → policy lifecycle
- claims-service → claim lifecycle
- NO cross-domain logic allowed

---

## 15. Anti-Patterns

- Shared business logic across services
- Direct DB access between services
- God services
- Synchronous chains across multiple services

---

## 16. Output Expectations

When generating code:

---
#
# Global settings.
#

spring:
  application:
    name: @project.artifactId@
  profiles:
    active: ${SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE:local}


---
#
# Local profile.
#

spring:
  cloud:
    config:
      enabled: false
  config:
    activate:
      on-profile: local


---
#
# Environments.
#

spring:
  cloud:
    config:
      fail-fast: false
      uri: ${SPRING_CONFIG_URI}
  config:
    activate:
      on-profile: ${MOMCORP_PROFILES}
